pubmed_links: [Uctepe et al. (2023) TRAPPC9-Related Intellectual Disability: Report of Two New Cases and Review of the Literature](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38058760/); [Al-Mubarak et al. (2021) Two Novel Compound Heterozygous Mutations in TRAPPC9 Reveal a Connection of Non-syndromic Intellectual Disability and ASD](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33719327/); [Marangi et al. (2019) Novel Compound Heterozygous Mutations in TRAPPC9 in Two Siblings With Autism and Intellectual Disability](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30853973/)
summary: TRAPPC9 mutations cause autosomal recessive intellectual disability (MRT13) with postnatal microcephaly and brain white matter abnormalities; among reported cases, all show intellectual disability and approximately 26% also meet criteria for autism spectrum disorder. (well-established)
